Instead of relying on the same mouse and keyboard inputs that have defined personal computing for 40 years, the company sees AI agents as a new interface that will let users command their systems and find information with natural language.And once those agents have their marching orders, they’ll need to set goals, call tools, evaluate the quality of their work, and refine it, potentially using local and cloud AI models to achieve those ends. Agents might also continue working on long-running tasks even when the user is away from their system or overnight. That all requires powerful, efficient hardware and lots of local memory.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: You may like Nvidia lays out RTX Spark roadmap for laptops and desktop PCs at Computex 2026 Nvidia's RTX Spark could caplitalize where Qualcomm's Arm-based efforts have not Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra weilds Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip with 128GB of RAM, 20 Arm CPU cores, and a Blackwell GPU To power all this AI reasoning in the new era of computing it envisions, Nvidia is unleashing the RTX Spark Superchip, a Windows on Arm platform more powerful and capable any other on the market, and a roadmap for the Spark family outlining the next three generations of technology.At full strength, this chip offers up to 20 Arm CPU cores, a Blackwell GPU with 6,144 CUDA cores, 128GB of LPDDR5X RAM, and up to 300 GB/s of memory bandwidth. That powerful CPU and GPU, connected over NVLink C2C, and the large memory pool give AI agents and 120-billion-parameter models plenty of power and space for long-running tasks with context lengths stretching to a million tokens, according to Nvidia.RTX Spark will power high-end laptops from partners including Dell, HP, Lenovo, Asus, and MSI -- and notably, a new Surface Ultra laptop from Microsoft. Nvidia says it’s worked with those partners to create “the most extraordinary laptops [they’ve] ever built,” with tandem OLED G-Sync displays, “all-day” battery life, premium aluminum chassis with large glass touchpads.Nvidia says that the incredible efficiency of the RTX Spark platform “transforms what a high-performance laptop looks like,” so buyers in the promised agentic AI age will no longer need to choose between high performance or thin chassis with long battery life. RTX Spark PCs will also deliver similar performance whether plugged in or unplugged, as we’ve come to expect from other Windows on Arm and Apple Silicon-powered systems.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsRTX Spark will also bring this agentic Windows on Arm experience to compact, powerful desktops in the vein of the DGX Spark. In total, Nvidia expects over 30 laptops and “10 or so” desktops to lead the charge when the platform launches.In addition to its agentic AI chops, Nvidia positions the RTX Spark Superchip as a creative and gaming powerhouse. The company promises the platform is good for “100 FPS 1440p gaming,” potentially enabled by DLSS 4.5 upscaling and Multi Frame Generation. And its large memory pool means creators can work with massive 3D projects and ultra-high resolution video files like 12K 4:2:2 content without running out of resources.To further the RTX Spark platform’s creative chops, Nvidia says it’s working with Adobe to rebuild the core of Photoshop, transforming it into a 100% GPU-accelerated application for RTX Spark. Those updates will enable new generative workflows, high-dynamic-range editing, and more natural brushing for artists. What to read next Nvidia and Microsoft tease "a new era of PC" — rumored N1X laptops could be Windows on Arm systems Computex 2026 Day Zero Wrap-Up Nvidia's N1/N1X chips leak ahead of Computex launch, up to 20 Arm cores and RTX 5070-tier graphics And Premiere is also getting a core overhaul that’s claimed to enable faster and more sophisticated AI workflows, editing, color, and effects. Adobe will also expose Model Context Protocol controls for AI agents to harness its products.In partnership with Microsoft, Nvidia is also helping to transform Windows into an agentic platform with its OpenShell framework and a “new set of security primitives” that form a set of guardrails, ensuring that local agents and models only have access to the tools and data the user grants them access to. Nvidia says Microsoft will reveal more details of this agentic AI transformation at its upcoming Build conference.RTX Spark systems will begin arriving in the fall of 2026, and we can't wait to dig into them to see whether Nvidia's backing will truly transform the Windows on Arm experience for the agentic AI era - or just make for a really great PC platform. Stay tuned. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jeffrey Kampman
history.titleZh: 英伟达在2026年台北电脑展上发布适用于笔记本电脑和台式
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年台北国际电脑展（Computex 2026）上，英伟达（Nvidia）首席执行官黄仁勋在主题演讲中正式发布了RTX Spark平台，这一平台旨在将Windows操作系统转变为一个支持人工智能代理（agentic AI）的平台。英伟达宣称RTX Spark是“有史以来最高效的平台”，并全力打造一流的Windows on Arm体验，将其视为个人计算的下一个前沿领域。

英伟达认为，AI代理正在重塑人与PC的交互方式。过去40年，个人计算一直依赖鼠标和键盘输入，而AI代理将成为一种全新的交互界面，用户可以通过自然语言指令操作系统和查找信息。一旦AI代理获得指令，它们将需要设定目标、调用工具、评估工作质量并进行优化，这一过程可能需要同时使用本地和云端AI模型。更重要的是，AI代理可以在用户离开系统或过夜时继续执行长时间运行的任务，这要求硬件具备强大的性能和效率，以及充足的本地内存。

为了支撑这一新计算时代的AI推理需求，英伟达推出了RTX Spark超级芯片，这是一个比市场上任何其他平台都更强大、更高效的Windows on Arm平台，并公布了未来三代RTX Spark家族的技术路线图。该芯片在满配状态下包含最多20个Arm CPU核心、一个拥有6,144个CUDA核心的Blackwell GPU、128GB LPDDR5X内存，以及高达300GB/s的内存带宽。英伟达表示，通过NVLink C2C高速互联技术连接的高性能CPU和GPU，加上大容量内存池，使得AI代理和拥有1,200亿参数的模型能够轻松处理长达百万token的上下文任务，从而获得充足的计算能力和存储空间。

RTX Spark将驱动来自戴尔、惠普、联想、华硕、微星等合作伙伴的高端笔记本电脑，值得注意的是，微软也将推出一款全新的Surface Ultra笔记本电脑。英伟达称，它与这些合作伙伴共同打造了“有史以来最卓越的笔记本电脑”，配备串联OLED G-Sync显示屏、全天续航、优质铝合金机身和大面积玻璃触控板。英伟达表示，RTX Spark平台的惊人效率“彻底改变了高性能笔记本电脑的形态”，在AI代理时代，用户无需再在高性能与轻薄机身及长续航之间做出妥协。此外，RTX Spark PC在插电和不插电状态下性能表现一致，这与我们已习惯的其他Windows on Arm和Apple Silicon系统类似。

除了笔记本电脑，RTX Spark还将为类似于DGX Spark那样紧凑而强大的桌面PC提供支持。英伟达预计，该平台首发时将推出超过30款笔记本电脑和“大约10款”桌面电脑。

在AI代理能力之外，英伟达还将RTX Spark超级芯片定位为创意和游戏领域的性能猛兽。该公司承诺，该平台能够实现“1440p分辨率下100 FPS”的游戏体验，这很可能得益于DLSS 4.5超分辨率和多帧生成技术。其大容量内存意味着创作者可以处理超大型3D项目和超高清视频文件（例如12K 4:2:2内容），而不会出现资源不足的情况。

为了进一步强化RTX Spark平台的创意能力，英伟达表示正在与Adobe合作，对Photoshop的核心进行重构，将其转变为完全由GPU加速的RTX Spark应用程序。这些更新将支持新的生成式工作流、高动态范围编辑以及更自然的画笔绘画体验。Premiere Pro也将进行核心重构，以实现更快、更复杂的AI工作流、编辑、调色和特效处理。Adobe还将开放模型上下文协议（Model Context Protocol）控制，使AI代理能够调用其产品功能。

在与微软的合作中，英伟达还通过其OpenShell框架和“一套新的安全原语”帮助将Windows转变为一个AI代理平台。这些安全原语形成了一套防护机制，确保本地AI代理和模型只能访问用户授予权限的工具和数据。英伟达透露，微软将在即将举行的Build开发者大会上公布更多关于这一AI代理转型的细节。

RTX Spark系统将于2026年秋季开始陆续上市。届时，我们将深入评测，验证英伟达的全力支持是否真的能将Windows on Arm体验带入AI代理时代，抑或仅仅是打造了一个出色的PC平台。

history.title: Intel Xeon 7 ‘Diamond Rapids’ CPUs officially launching in 2027 on Intel 18A-P — next-gen P-core Xeon features PCIe 6.0, 50% higher core counts, and twice the memory bandwidth
history.description: Intel has officially confirmed its next-gen Xeon 7 Diamond Rapids CPUs are coming in 2027, featuring 50% higher core counts and twice the memory bandwidth of Xeon 6 in a bid to compete against AMD’s upcoming EPYC Venice CPUs.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel has confirmed several details about its next-generation Xeon 7 CPUs, codenamed Diamond Rapids, which are now officially slated to launch in 2027. Announcing its E-core-only Xeon 6+ chips at Computex, Intel teased that Diamond Rapids will support PCIe 6.0, pack 50% more cores than Xeon 6, and double the memory bandwidth. Intel is building Diamond Rapids chips on the Intel 18A-P node, which is a refined version of 18A that Intel demoed just last month.Although Intel never formally announced a release window for Xeon 7, we originally expected to see the chips this year – a timeframe that became increasingly unlikely as news about Diamond Rapids dried up. Now, Intel has officially confirmed Diamond Rapids is arriving next year, meaning AMD will have a head start with its next-gen EPYC Venice CPUs built on the Zen 6 architecture, which are still (at the moment) slated for release this year. (Image credit: Intel)Like Venice, Intel has confirmed Diamond Rapids will support PCIe 6.0, as well as double the memory bandwidth of Granite Rapids. Last year, Intel confirmed it canceled an 8-channel memory variant of Diamond Rapids to focus exclusively on the 16-channel design. Granite Rapids-AP (12-channel) topped out at 614 GB/s of memory bandwidth, while Granite Rapids-SP (8-channel) topped out at 409 GB/s. Depending on the comparison point — Intel didn’t clarify — you’re looking at topline memory bandwidth of at least 1.2 TB/s or 818 GB/s, respectively. Second-generation MRDIMM support, however, means that bandwidth could climb to upwards of 1.6 TB/s.Latest Videos From You may like Intel's upcoming Xeon 7 "Diamond Rapids" server CPUs reportedly delayed to 2027 Intel Xeon 6+ ‘Clearwater Forest’ puts 18A in the data center with up to 288 cores, 576 MB of L3 cache Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 6+ CPU We can do similar math with core counts, looking at the top-end 6980P from Granite Rapid-AP at 128 cores. A 50% increase in core count brings us to 192 cores. Diamond Rapids has been rumored to climb up to 256 cores, with a 512-core dense version planned later. Intel is suggesting those rumors are false, though no hard specifications are confirmed yet.The big question is what microarchitecture those cores will use, and if they’ll support Hyper-Threading. Intel removed Hyper-Threading from the Lion Cove P-cores in Lunar Lake and Arrow Lake, and kept it out of the Cougar Cove P-cores in Panther Lake. Naturally, if Xeon 7 uses either of those P-core microarchitectures, we’d also expect the chips to lack Hyper-Threading. Adding to the speculation were some comments Intel made in its January earnings call, where it said that it “will also reintroduce multi-threading back into our data center road map.”Recently, however, Intel documents have suggested Diamond Rapids will use Panther Cove, an as-of-yet unreleased microarchitecture. We should know for sure what’s going on under the hood of Diamond Rapids soon. "We expect to share more on Diamond Rapids in the late summer at Hot Chips, so stay tuned there,” an Intel spokesperson shared in a Q&A with Tom’s Hardware. Perhaps the most significant reveal in this tease is that Diamond Rapids is using Intel 18A-P. We’ve known for a while that the first 18A CPUs in the data center would be Xeon 6+, which Intel has now officially launched. 18A-P is a revision of 18A, but Intel has already demonstrated that it’s quite a significant revision.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIntel claims 18A-P delivers 9% higher performance at the same power as 18A, or an 18% power reduction at the same performance level. Intel says it also improved reliability and tweaked voltage behavior, making 18A-P a much more mature revision of 18A, likely in a bid to attract external customers for Intel Foundry.Intel is facing off against AMD’s Zen 6 Venice CPUs, which we expect to learn more about at AMD’s Advancing AI event in July. So far, AMD has confirmed that Venice will launch with up to 256 cores, 1.6 TB/s of memory bandwidth per socket, and a 70% jump in gen-on-gen performance. There’s still a lot we don’t know about Venice and Diamond Rapids, but from the initial teases, Team Red is looking like the leader.That would make sense. Although Diamond Rapids is a significant release for Intel, the company has continually reiterated the importance of Coral Rapids, the generation that will follow Xeon 7 on Intel’s road map. We expect to see Coral Rapids in 2028, featuring SMT, and Intel has said that it’s looking into ways to accelerate the Coral Rapids rollout. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jake Roach
history.titleZh: Intel 至强 7 ‘Diamond Rapids’ CPU 将于 2027 年正式在 Intel 18A-P
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔在2024年台北国际电脑展（Computex）上正式确认了其下一代Xeon 7系列服务器CPU（代号Diamond Rapids）的关键细节，并宣布该芯片预计于2027年推出。此前业界普遍预期Diamond Rapids会在2025年面世，但英特尔官方的最新表态将时间推迟至2027年，这意味着AMD凭借基于Zen 6架构的新一代EPYC Venice CPU（仍按计划于2025年发布）将在服务器市场获得先发优势。

**核心技术创新与工艺节点**

Diamond Rapids将基于英特尔18A-P制程节点，这是此前在数据中心市场首次亮相的18A节点的改进版本。18A-P在性能与功耗上均有所优化：在同等功耗下性能提升9%，或在保持相同性能水平时功耗降低18%。此外，该节点在可靠性和电压特性上也进行了调整，使其成熟度更高，旨在吸引更多外部客户使用英特尔的代工服务。

在微架构方面，Diamond Rapids预计将采用尚未发布的Panther Cove核心架构。一个关键争论点是它是否支持超线程（Hyper-Threading）。英特尔在Lunar Lake和Arrow Lake的Lion Cove P核以及Panther Lake的Cougar Cove P核中都移除了超线程，但今年1月的财报电话会议中，英特尔表示将“重新将多线程引入数据中心路线图”。近期文件暗示Diamond Rapids将使用Panther Cove架构，不过是否恢复超线程仍需等待官方确认。英特尔透露，将在今年夏末的Hot Chips会议上分享更多Diamond Rapids的底层细节。

**性能参数与对比数据**

Diamond Rapids在I/O和内存带宽上实现重大飞跃。它将支持PCIe 6.0，同时内存带宽相比上一代Granite Rapids翻倍。Granite Rapids-AP（12通道）最高内存带宽为614 GB/s，Granite Rapids-SP（8通道）最高为409 GB/s。Diamond Rapids取消了此前规划的8通道版本，专注于16通道设计，因此理论最高内存带宽至少可达1.2 TB/s（相比Granite Rapids-AP）或818 GB/s（相比Granite Rapids-SP）。得益于对第二代MRDIMM（多路复用器双列直插式内存模组）的支持，其带宽有望进一步攀升至超过1.6 TB/s。

核心数量方面，以Granite Rapids-AP最高128核为基准，50%的核心数增长意味着Diamond Rapids将拥有约192核。此前曾有传闻称其可达256核甚至512核的密集版本，但英特尔暗示这些传闻不准确，不过并未给出具体规格。与AMD下一代Venice CPU相比，AMD已确认Venice将提供最高256核、每插槽1.6 TB/s内存带宽以及一代内70%的性能提升，从目前透露的信息看，AMD在核心数和部分带宽指标上暂时领先。

**技术影响与应用场景**

Diamond Rapids的推出对于英特尔在数据中心市场的竞争至关重要。它不仅是首款采用Intel 18A-P节点的Xeon 7系列芯片，还通过大幅提升核心数与内存带宽，旨在应对AI训练、高性能计算（HPC）以及大规模虚拟化等对算力和内存吞吐要求极高的应用场景。16通道内存和PCIe 6.0的支持，使其特别适合需要高速数据交换的服务器环境，如数据库、实时分析和云服务。

尽管Diamond Rapids是英特尔的重要产品，但公司已反复强调后续的Coral Rapids（预计2028年推出，支持同步多线程SMT）的战略地位，并正研究加速其推出的方法。这意味着Diamond Rapids可能被视为过渡一代，但其在工艺成熟度和技术规格上的显著提升，仍将是英特尔在2027年前后对抗AMD Zen 6架构的关键武器。英特尔也希望通过Diamond Rapids的发布，展示其Intel 18A-P工艺的商业化能力，为旗下代工业务争取更多外部客户。
